resource.name: sessions/9936753553469226169
resource.title: Task 5: Memory Objects — Player & Core (Batch 1)
resource.prompt:
IMPORTANT: You have access to the context7 MCP server. Before writing ANY Rust code, use context7 to look up the latest documentation for the crates you are using. Look up: windows-rs, tokio, thiserror, bitflags, byteorder. Always verify crate APIs against context7 docs.

IMPORTANT: The following types are already defined in this repo on main — import them, do NOT redefine them:
- crate::errors::{Result, WizWalkerError}
- crate::types::{XYZ, Orient, Color, Keycode}
- crate::memory::memory_object::{MemoryObject, MemoryReader, DynamicMemoryObject}

Port the first batch of WizWalker memory object wrappers from Python to Rust.

Source files (all from https://github.com/LaurenzLikeThat/wizwalker/blob/development/wizwalker/memory/memory_objects/):
- actor_body.py → objects/actor_body.rs
- client_object.py → objects/client_object.rs
- core_object.py → objects/core_object.rs
- core_template.py → objects/core_template.rs
- game_client.py → objects/game_client.rs
- client_zone.py → objects/client_zone.rs
- enums.py → objects/enums.rs

Each wraps a MemoryObject (the trait is already in memory/memory_object.rs) with async methods reading typed values at specific offsets. CRITICAL: Preserve ALL offset values exactly. Convert Python IntEnum/Flag to Rust enums with repr(i32) or bitflags! macro. Update objects/mod.rs.
